refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these were separated into distinct sub-FRs. Additionally, the FR focuses on what the system must do (derive requirements) without specifying how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can identify and extract performance requirements based solely on the worksite mission context without reference to implementation details.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
The NGHE solution shall record performance requirements derived from the worksite mission context.
The NGHE solution shall manage performance requirements derived from the worksite mission context.

candidate_dps: Define performance metrics schema based on mission context
Map worksite mission inputs to performance target parameters
Generate documented performance targets with traceability
Maintain historical records of baseline and calibration updates
selected_dp.selected_dp: Map worksite mission inputs to performance target parameters

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates the mapping function from documentation and history maintenance, supporting modularity and independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes worksite mission inputs are well-defined and accessible
Risk of incomplete mapping if mission context changes dynamically
Requires consistent update mechanism for baseline and calibration history
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating documentation and history maintenance DPs to further reduce coupling and clarify responsibilities.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system responsible for processing mission inputs and mapping them to performance target parameters, integrating data and control logic across subsystems.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data from the vehicle and environment to inform performance targets.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission input commands and objectives from higher-level control or operator.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends processed performance target parameters to other modules or external systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the DP is central to the overall solution mapping mission inputs to performance targets; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Partial containment possible but would require diffusion of mission input processing outside vehicle control logic, increasing coupling.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and would not fully contain the DP; significant diffusion expected.
Safety Services are peripheral to mission input mapping; poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP as it directly manages the mapping of mission inputs to performance targets, centralizing related logic and minimizing diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential complexity within NGHE Solution may grow if too many functions are centralized, risking reduced modularity.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation must be well-defined to avoid hidden dependencies.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP clearly addresses the FR by specifying the mapping of worksite mission inputs to performance target parameters, directly fulfilling the requirement to derive performance requirements from mission context.

dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 1), indicating the DP is well centralized within this module. Other modules show higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the appropriate IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module receives mission inputs via defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking) and outputs performance targets via Network Link, enabling traceability and documentation of performance targets as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate (NGHE Solution) is clearly defined and aligns well with the DP, ensuring clear allocation of responsibility for the FR within the IM layer.

recommended_revisions: Monitor complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module to maintain modularity and consider refactoring if functionality becomes too centralized.
Ensure interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules are explicitly defined and managed to prevent hidden dependencies and maintain clear module boundaries.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully with clear mappings, high containment, low diffusion, and feasible verification. The NGHE Solution module is well defined as the implementation module with clear ownership and interfaces. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining modularity and clear boundaries is essential, but current decomposition is adequate. Recommended revisions focus on monitoring and interface management rather than further decomposition.

revise.why: Monitor complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module and ensure interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules are explicitly defined to maintain modularity and prevent hidden dependencies.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can handle the current functional scope without excessive complexity.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules are stable and well documented.
open_risks: Potential complexity growth within NGHE Solution module over time.
Risk of hidden dependencies if interfaces are not properly managed.
evidence_needed: Ongoing complexity metrics for NGHE Solution module.
Interface specifications and change logs for Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
